19:Ta03,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# 🚀 AWS Prompt the Planet Challenge

## 1. Competition Overview

The AWS Prompt the Planet Challenge is a global competition aimed at encouraging creators worldwide to contribute prompts that make cloud infrastructure accessible to everyone. This hackathon invites participants to submit prompts that simplify AWS infrastructure deployment, solve real-world challenges, and guide users step-by-step with examples and explanations.

### Key Focus Areas
- **Prompts That Simplify**: Instructions to deploy infrastructure, solve problems, or automate tasks on AWS.
- **Solutions for Real Challenges**: Security setup, cost management, application deployment, data handling, and automation.
- **Clear & Actionable**: Prompts that guide users with examples and explanations.

### Example Prompts
- Day 1 AWS Foundation Setup
- MVSP-Compliant Infrastructure Builder
- Karpenter + KEDA Cost-Optimized EKS
- AWS Security Baseline Terraform Kit
- RAG Chatbot with Claude

### Prize
- $50,000 in AWS Activate Credits
- 10 winners receive $5,000 each
- Featured placement in the AWS Startups Prompt Library

### Eligibility
- Open to everyone: students, developers, founders, researchers, hobbyists, career changers.
- Multiple submissions encouraged.

### Timeline
- Submission window: March 1 - June 1, 2026 (90 days)

### Submission Requirements
- Complete prompt with verbatim instructions.
- Context & Documentation: prerequisites, use case, expected outcome, troubleshooting tips.
- AWS Services & Best Practices: alignment with AWS Well-Architected Framework.

### Quality Standards
- Clear & Actionable
- Production-Ready
- Well-Documented
- Best Practice Aligned

### Submission Process
- Submission window: March 1 - June 1, 2026
- Submissions reviewed for completeness, clarity, technical accuracy, production readiness, and alignment with AWS best practices.

### Tools
- Kiro IDE encouraged for use throughout the challenge.
- Other agentic coding assistants like Claude Code are welcome.

### Why Use Kiro?
- Agentic Coding: AI agents for complex development tasks.
- Intelligent Steering: Advanced controls to guide AI behavior.
- Rapid Iteration: Quick prototyping and testing with AI assistance.
- Production-Ready Code: High-quality, maintainable code for deployment.

### Important Notes
- Kiro use is not required for participation.
- Encouraged to use Kiro and other agentic coding tools throughout the challenge.

### Your Ideas Can Help Thousands
Every prompt submitted becomes part of a global library that helps developers worldwide build on AWS."])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"12:["$","div",null,{"className":"min-h-screen bg-background","children":[["$","div",null,{"className":"container mx-auto px-4 py-8"}],["$","$L18",null,{"competition":{"id":"dorahacksawsprompttheplanet","title":"AWS Prompt the Planet Challenge","introduction":"The AWS Prompt the Planet Challenge invites creators to submit prompts simplifying AWS infrastructure, addressing real-world challenges, and guiding users with examples. class="text-3xl font-bold mb-4">🚀 AWS Prompt the Planet Challenge</h2>
<h2 class="text-2xl font-semibold mb-3">1. Competition Overview</h2>
<p class="mb-4 leading-relaxed whitespace-pre-line">The AWS Prompt the Planet Challenge is a global competition aimed at encouraging creators worldwide to contribute prompts that make cloud infrastructure accessible to everyone. This hackathon invites participants to submit prompts that simplify AWS infrastructure deployment, solve real-world challenges, and guide users step-by-step with examples and explanations.</p>
<h3 class="text-xl font-medium mb-2">Key Focus Areas</h3>
<ul class="list-disc list-inside mb-4 space-y-1">
<li class="mb-1"><strong>Prompts That Simplify</strong>: Instructions to deploy infrastructure, solve problems, or automate tasks on AWS.</li>
<li class="mb-1"><strong>Solutions for Real Challenges</strong>: Security setup, cost management, application deployment, data handling, and automation.</li>
<li class="mb-1"><strong>Clear & Actionable</strong>: Prompts that guide users with examples and explanations.</li>
</ul>
<h3 class="text-xl font-medium mb-2">Example Prompts</h3>
<ul class="list-disc list-inside mb-4 space-y-1">
<li class="mb-1">Day 1 AWS Foundation Setup</li>
<li class="mb-1">MVSP-Compliant Infrastructure Builder</li>
<li class="mb-1">Karpenter + KEDA Cost-Optimized EKS</li>
<li class="mb-1">AWS Security Baseline Terraform Kit</li>
<li class="mb-1">RAG Chatbot with Claude</li>
</ul>
<h3 class="text-xl font-medium mb-2">Prize</h3>
<ul class="list-disc list-inside mb-4 space-y-1">
<li class="mb-1">$50,000 in AWS Activate Credits</li>
<li class="mb-1">10 winners receive $5,000 each</li>
<li class="mb-1">Featured placement in the AWS Startups Prompt Library</li>
</ul>
<h3 class="text-xl font-medium mb-2">Eligibility</h3>
<ul class="list-disc list-inside mb-4 space-y-1">
<li class="mb-1">Open to everyone: students, developers, founders, researchers, hobbyists, career changers.</li>
<li class="mb-1">Multiple submissions encouraged.</li>
</ul>
<h3 class="text-xl font-medium mb-2">Timeline</h3>
<ul class="list-disc list-inside mb-4 space-y-1">
<li class="mb-1">Submission window: March 1 - June 1, 2026 (90 days)</li>
</ul>
<h3 class="text-xl font-medium mb-2">Submission Requirements</h3>
<ul class="list-disc list-inside mb-4 space-y-1">
<li class="mb-1">Complete prompt with verbatim instructions.</li>
<li class="mb-1">Context & Documentation: prerequisites, use case, expected outcome, troubleshooting tips.</li>
<li class="mb-1">AWS Services & Best Practices: alignment with AWS Well-Architected Framework.</li>
</ul>
<h3 class="text-xl font-medium mb-2">Quality Standards</h3>
<ul class="list-disc list-inside mb-4 space-y-1">
<li class="mb-1">Clear & Actionable</li>
<li class="mb-1">Production-Ready</li>
<li class="mb-1">Well-Documented</li>
<li class="mb-1">Best Practice Aligned</li>
</ul>
<h3 class="text-xl font-medium mb-2">Submission Process</h3>
<ul class="list-disc list-inside mb-4 space-y-1">
<li class="mb-1">Submission window: March 1 - June 1, 2026</li>
<li class="mb-1">Submissions reviewed for completeness, clarity, technical accuracy, production readiness, and alignment with AWS best practices.</li>
</ul>
<h3 class="text-xl font-medium mb-2">Tools</h3>
<ul class="list-disc list-inside mb-4 space-y-1">
<li class="mb-1">Kiro IDE encouraged for use throughout the challenge.</li>
<li class="mb-1">Other agentic coding assistants like Claude Code are welcome.</li>
</ul>
<h3 class="text-xl font-medium mb-2">Why Use Kiro?</h3>
<ul class="list-disc list-inside mb-4 space-y-1">
<li class="mb-1">Agentic Coding: AI agents for complex development tasks.</li>
<li class="mb-1">Intelligent Steering: Advanced controls to guide AI behavior.</li>
<li class="mb-1">Rapid Iteration: Quick prototyping and testing with AI assistance.</li>
<li class="mb-1">Production-Ready Code: High-quality, maintainable code for deployment.</li>
</ul>
<h3 class="text-xl font-medium mb-2">Important Notes</h3>
<ul class="list-disc list-inside mb-4 space-y-1">
<li class="mb-1">Kiro use is not required for participation.</li>
<li class="mb-1">Encouraged to use Kiro and other agentic coding tools throughout the challenge.</li>
</ul>
